text:
Cuzco (クズコ, Kuzuko) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 203) is a Greed Island player, one of the last known players to join Nickes' Alliance, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 132 and the only known alliance member to survive aside from Abengane. 

Appearance

Cuzco is a gaunt-looking man with an almond-shaped head and long, unkempt hair. While attending the Southernpiece Auction, he was clean-shaven and wore a suit and tie, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 but during his time in Greed Island, he had facial stubble and wore a plain long-sleeved shirt, pants, and shoes.

Plot

Greed Island arc

Cuzco attends the Southernpiece Auction as an attendee and notes that the JoyStation Console's power light is on, but it's not plugged in. 

Sometime after being hired by Battera and entering the game, Cuzco is among a group of new players who were convinced to join Nickes' Alliance, presumably by Contarch. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 131 The recruits wait in front of the Shiso Tree for a senior member known as Jispa to arrive. The moment he does, Jispa instantly uses an "Accompany" to transport them to another alliance member named Kosofftro, who sits somewhere near the alliance base. As the new recruits are led to the base by Jispa, he divulges to them that their first task is to collect certain spell cards in Masadora for the alliance. At the alliance base, the recruits are instructed to memorize a list of spell cards within an hour. 

When the alliance is close to collecting all of the cards in the game, Genthru reveals himself to be the Bomber and claims that he has placed bombs on all of the alliance members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 142 After Genthru leaves the base, Cuzco appears flabbergasted along with Redwood. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 143 

Cuzco's name is mentioned in Gon's binder and has a bright dot next to it, meaning that he somehow managed to survive the Bombers' massacre of most of the alliance. Goreinu mentions him as one of the Hunters hired to play the game. 

Abilities & Powers

Cuzco is capable enough to have passed the notoriously difficult Hunter Exam and has access to all the privileges granted by his status as a Hunter. Since most of the alliance members joined because they lacked combat abilities, it can be assumed that Cuzco also has none. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Cuzco is capable of utilizing Nen, and it can be assumed that he is moderately skilled at it since he was approved by Tsezguerra. Given the fact that he manages to survive the Bomb Devils' Countdown, it is possible that he possesses an ability to have it removed. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Cuzco's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder and recognized by Goreinu, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook. 
Cuzco's name is the same as the city located in Peru.
